boundary: Even if this passes, it is not full Gap A closure by itself. The rank law and size-profile law still require independent native/provenance justification, relay mediators are not uniquely selected, station fields are not tested, and the full role-labeled shared_B universe is not yet derived.

generation_rules.quotient_support_rule: every cycle step must have native G60 mod15 residue-pair support
generation_rules.relay_obligation_rule: candidate residue set must intersect every same-state unlifted relay-hit set from artifact 011

interpretation: This is a direct Gap A assault on the anchor selector. It stops assuming the observed anchor residue sets and instead generates candidates from state bits, C-relay obligations, native mod15 G60 quotient support, and the rank law. If the selected candidates match the observed anchor paths in all four states, the anchor-selector subproblem moves from observed residue-set assumption to a native/provenance candidate law.
